buyingChecks.title: Buying This X2 18d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes (critical for B47 longevity)
* EGR recall work completed (check with BMW dealer)
* No engine warning lights (DPF and EGR health)
* Evidence of regular longer runs (DPF health)
avoidIf: * No service history — the B47 is sensitive to oil quality
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — could be DPF, EGR, or sensor issues
* Short-journey ownership history — DPF and EGR issues likely
* Any history of timing chain issues (rare on B47 but possible at very high mileage)

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X2 18d is BMW's entry-level diesel in the sporty F39 crossover, powered by the 2.0-litre B47 four-cylinder diesel. The B47 is a significant improvement over the N47 — the timing chain was relocated to the front, making it both more durable and easier to service. Owners regularly report well over 120,000 miles without major issues. Common issues include EGR valve clogging, DPF clogging, and a crankshaft sensor software recall. None of these are catastrophic if caught early. With typical X2 values ranging from £12,000 to £25,000, an engine replacement is a significant investment — but the B47 is generally reliable when properly maintained.

cta.label: → Compare X2 18d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X2 18d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for smooth start; any rattling indicates timing chain wear (rare on B47 but possible) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine variant (B47C20B or B47C20U1) and oil-change discipline
* EGR System — Check for any performance issues or warning lights; EGR clogging is common
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear
* Recall Status — Check with BMW dealer that crankshaft sensor software recall has been completed
* Turbo Health — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or hesitation?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes read; unresolved issues may indicate DPF, EGR or sensor problems
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a BMW X2 18d engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned X2 18d engine typically costs £3,200–£5,000 supply only. Fitting adds £800–£1,300 depending on the specialist. A reconditioned engine fully installed typically costs around £1,800–£2,800. A new OEM replacement engine can cost £2,500–£4,000+, with labour pushing the total above £5,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your X2.

items.question: Is the BMW X2 18d engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes — the B47 is a significant improvement over the N47. The timing chain was relocated to the front of the engine, making it both more durable and easier to service. The B47 is generally regarded as a strong and dependable engine, capable of handling high mileage if properly maintained. Owners regularly report well over 120,000 miles without major issues. Common issues include EGR valve clogging, DPF clogging, and a crankshaft sensor software recall.

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW X2 18d?
items.answer: The most common issues are EGR valve clogging (particularly on cars driven mainly in urban environments), DPF clogging (especially on cars driven mostly in the city), and a crankshaft sensor software fault that was subject to a recall. Timing chain issues are far less common than on the N47. None of these are catastrophic if caught early.

items.question: Is it worth replacing an X2 18d engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Depends on the generation and vehicle condition. Against a well-kept F39 X2 worth £12,000–£20,000, a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 20–35% of the car's value — often worth it. Against a tired example with significant body issues, the same recon may approach our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. B47-engined cars are generally reliable, so engine replacement is less common than on older BMW engines.

items.question: How do I find my BMW X2 18d engine code?
items.answer: sDrive 18d (2018–2023): B47C20U1. xDrive 18d (2018–2023): B47C20B. Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation.

items.question: Can I repair the B47 timing chain, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: The B47 timing chain is far more durable than the N47, with the chain relocated to the front of the engine. Timing chain wear is far less frequent than on the N47. However, if wear does occur at very high mileage, the chain and guides must be replaced. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.
